summary: Dependency-confusion recon beacon exfiltrates host identity on install
details: On `npm install`, the preinstall script (`node index.js`) collects host identity (`os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo().username`, `__dirname`, `process.cwd()`) and exfiltrates it via two channels: (1) a DNS A-record lookup to a hex-encoded subdomain of `d8jbmnsqcfu78dfs8vdg34ohqhirb4pbg.oast.live` (Interactsh out-of-band canary infrastructure), and (2) an HTTP POST of the JSON payload to the hardcoded bare IP `http://172.201.213.59:9090/c`. Errors are swallowed so the beacon stays silent. The package metadata (name `@klapp-login-platform/routes`, version `99.0.0`, description `security research`, placeholder author) is the canonical shape of a dependency-confusion attack: a public package published under an internal-looking scope with an inflated version to outrank private resolutions and capture installs from misconfigured registries.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
